key_points: Algebra allows complex number puzzles to be solved using symbolic representation
Patterns in number pyramids can lead to binomial-style expressions
Digit manipulation problems reveal divisibility properties
Algebraic reasoning helps compare products and determine optimal arrangements
Divisibility tricks can often be explained through factorisation
examples: Four-level pyramid with base a, b, c, d gives top value a + 3b + 3c + d
Difference between 74 and 47 equals 27 which is divisible by 9
Sum of number and its reverse such as 34 + 43 = 77 which is divisible by 11
Repeating number 345345 can be written as 345 × 1001
formulas: Four-row pyramid top expression → a + 3b + 3c + d
Sum of number and its reverse → 11(a + b)
exam_tips: Write algebraic representation before solving puzzles
Use factorisation to prove divisibility properties
Look for hidden patterns in digit arrangements
